name: Vyasatirtha
dates_notes: Vijayanagara court records (Kṛṣṇadevarāya's preceptor); dates very well-established.

dates_evidence.description: Vijayanagara royal records, especially Kṛṣṇadevarāya's preceptor-status documents.
Manuscript colophons across the Nyāyāmṛta and Tarka-Tāṇḍava.

lineage_polemical.note: Vyāsatīrtha's Nyāyāmṛta is the structural target of Madhusūdana's Advaita-Siddhi.

The Tarka-Tāṇḍava turns Navya-Nyāya inferential machinery — Gaṅgeśa's vyāpti, avacchedaka, paryāpti — against Naiyāyika epistemology itself, demonstrating that the Mādhva positions can be cast in this idiom while the Naiyāyika ones cannot be defended in it.

core_thesis:
Vyāsatīrtha is the architect of *Navya-Dvaita*: Madhva's *Tattvavāda* restated in the technical idiom of *Navya-Nyāya* and pressed to its dialectical limit. The positive metaphysics is straightforwardly Mādhva — *svatantra-paratantra* (independent / dependent) hierarchy, *pañca-bheda* (the five real differences), *viśeṣa* (the self-distinguishing relational element), *bimba-pratibimba* (original-and-image), *sākṣi* as a *pramāṇa* in its own right, Viṣṇu as the sole *svatantra-tattva*. The distinctive contribution is methodological.

Vyāsatīrtha engineers Mādhva positions as inferences in formal *Navya-Nyāya* form: explicit *vyāpti*-statement (universal concomitance), *upādhi*-tests (defeater-checks for the *vyāpti*), *avacchedaka*-conditions (delimitation of the predicate's locus). The technical apparatus is inherited from Gaṅgeśa's *Tattva-Cintāmaṇi*, which had reset the standard of inferential rigour in late-thirteenth-century Mithilā. Vyāsatīrtha's claim is that Mādhva positions can be cast in this idiom without doctrinal loss, and that, once so cast, they are at least as well-defended as the Advaita and Naiyāyika alternatives.

The *Nyāyāmṛta* is the principal output. It dismantles Advaitic *mithyātva* under each of its five canonical *lakṣaṇa*s and supplies positive *anumāna*s for *bheda*. The *Tarka-Tāṇḍava* turns the same machinery against Naiyāyika epistemology. Madhusūdana's *Advaita-Siddhi* is the structural reply that *Nyāyāmṛta* compelled; the cycle *Nyāyāmṛta → Advaita-Siddhi → Tarangiṇī → Laghu-Candrikā* is the longest sustained inter-school dialectic of late Vedānta.

engaged_works.title: Nyayamrta
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored; the most sophisticated Dvaita critique of Advaitic mithyātva.
engaged_works.summary:
The Dvaita anti-Advaita polemic in fully *Navya-Nyāya* idiom. Structured in four *paricchedas*, the *Nyāyāmṛta* dismantles the Advaitic *mithyātva*-thesis under each of its five canonical *lakṣaṇa*s, then mounts positive inferences for *bheda*.

The destructive labour comes first. Each of the five Advaitic *lakṣaṇa*s (*sad-asad-vilakṣaṇatva*, *jñāna-nivartyatva*, *sat-pratiyogika-bheda*, *abhāva-vilakṣaṇatva*, *svāśraya-niṣṭha-atyantābhāva-pratiyogitva*) is shown to either overextend (apply to *Brahman* itself), under-apply (fail to apply to *prapañca*, the world it was meant to characterise), or carry an internal contradiction.

The positive labour is concentrated in the *anumāna-pariccheda*. The central inference for *bheda* runs (in compressed form): *vimataṃ jīvāt bhinnaṃ, ghaṭa-ādīnāṃ tato bhedavat, taduktasya pramāṇa-niścayāt* — "the disputed [item, sc. the world or *Brahman*-as-distinct-from-the-*jīva*] is different from the *jīva*, like the difference of pots and the rest from the *jīva*, given the determination of authoritative knowledge to that effect." The *vyāpti* is: wherever an item is determined by an authoritative *pramāṇa* to differ from a relatum, the item differs. The *pakṣa* is the disputed item. The *hetu* is *pramāṇa-niścaya* (the determination of *pramāṇa*). The *udāharaṇa* is the case of pots, where *bheda* from the *jīva* is uncontroversial.

Vyāsatīrtha then anticipates the Advaitin defeater. The Advaitin will reply that the *pramāṇa* in question, if itself *mithyā*, cannot establish a *pāramārthika* difference. Vyāsatīrtha's response is that the Advaitin's own argument for *mithyātva* of *pramāṇa* presupposes a *pramāṇa* operating in *pāramārthika* mode (the very *anumāna* by which *mithyātva* is inferred), and so the defeater self-undercuts. The dialectic is run with explicit *upādhi*-tests at each step.

Madhusūdana's *Advaita-Siddhi* is the structural reply; the cycle *Nyāyāmṛta → Advaita-Siddhi → Tarangiṇī → Laghu-Candrikā → Nyāyāmṛta-Kaṇṭakoddhāra* runs through the seventeenth century.

engaged_works.title: Tarka-Tandava
engaged_works.summary:
Vyāsatīrtha's frontal engagement with *Navya-Nyāya*, most directly with Gaṅgeśa's *Tattva-Cintāmaṇi* tradition. The work proceeds *prakaraṇa* by *prakaraṇa* through Naiyāyika positions on the definition of *pramāṇa*, on *pratyakṣa* (perception), on *anumāna* (with particular attention to the *vyāpti-grahaṇa* problem, how universal concomitance is grasped), on *upamāna* (analogy), and on *śabda* (testimony). The idiom is the same Navya idiom the Naiyāyikas use.

Three constructive moves are particularly influential. First, the treatment of *kevalānvayī* inferences (those whose major term has no counter-instance) is recast so that *Īśvara*-inferences in Mādhva form survive Gaṅgeśa-style scrutiny. Second, *pratyakṣa* is analysed in terms compatible with *sākṣi-pramāṇa*: the witness-faculty is preserved as a self-luminous source whose verdicts are not displaced by mediated cognition. Third, the Naiyāyika treatment of *abhāva* (absence) as an autonomous *padārtha* (category) is refused; *abhāva* is treated as a mode of the *adhikaraṇa* (locus), which spares the school the regress-problems the autonomous-*padārtha* construal generates.

The opening *prāmāṇyavāda* is the test-case for the method. Vyāsatīrtha defends *svataḥ-prāmāṇya* (intrinsic validity of cognition) against the Naiyāyika *parataḥ-prāmāṇya* (extrinsic validity). The defence has two pieces: a compact *lakṣaṇa* (*jñāna-janaka-mātra-adhīna-janmatvaṃ svatastvam*, "intrinsicness is origination dependent solely on the producer of the cognition"), and an inference for the intrinsic graspability of validity that runs on a Navya-Nyāya pattern.

Where the *Nyāyāmṛta* engages Advaita, the *Tāṇḍava* engages the Naiyāyikas. Rāghavendra's *Pariṃala* is the principal sub-commentary.

engaged_works.title: Tatparya-Chandrika
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored sub-commentary on Jayatīrtha's Tattva-Prakāśikā.
engaged_works.summary:
A sub-sub-commentary on Jayatīrtha's *Tattva-Prakāśikā*, and so a third-order text on Madhva's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya*. The full stack is *sūtra* → Madhva's *bhāṣya* → Jayatīrtha's *Tattva-Prakāśikā* → Vyāsatīrtha's *Tātparya-Candrikā*; Rāghavendra's *Candrikā-Prakāśa* and other late *prakāśa*-glosses build on the *Candrikā* in turn.

The text moves through the *sūtra*s *adhikaraṇa* by *adhikaraṇa*, taking each of Jayatīrtha's glosses and doing three things with it. First, it defends the gloss against opposed readings developed in the intervening generations — especially the Appayya-line attacks on Madhva's authenticity as a Vedāntin and the Vivaraṇa-line refinements of Advaitic exegesis. Second, it extends the gloss into *Navya-Nyāya* form where Jayatīrtha had argued in older *prācīna* style; the formal apparatus that the *Nyāyāmṛta* deploys is here applied to the exegetical front. Third, it draws explicit connections to the *Anuvyākhyāna*–*Nyāya-Sudhā* sequence, so that the school's commentarial layers cohere as a single argued position rather than as four texts on the same topic.

The text is less polemical than the *Nyāyāmṛta* and *Tāṇḍava*. Its function is exegetical-consolidating. For the school's reading of the *Brahma-Sūtra* itself, it is the standard reference.

pada_analysis.gloss: whose infinite auspicious qualities are declared

key_passages.english_close: By self-valid āmnāyas. That is, [Vedas] devoid of an originating author. Reverently — that is, having infinite auspicious qualities thus declared. I worship the consort of Śrī.
key_passages.why_this_passage: This opening verse is not merely devotional ornament: it condenses Vyāsatīrtha's epistemic and theological commitments in one place, making Veda self-valid and authorless while presenting the Lord as the bearer of infinite auspicious qualities. Given that the accessible on-disk corpus for this run is commentary-inclusive and not a clean critical base text throughout, the work's own invocatory verse is one of the safest representative loci to anchor the selection.
key_passages.source_edition: GRETIL plaintext (`sa_vyAsatIrtha-tarkatANDava-comm.txt`), converted 2020-07-31 from the Sansknet Project source; includes Rāghavendra Tīrtha's Nyāyadīpa commentary.

key_passages.locus_long: Tarka-Tāṇḍava, pariccheda 1, prāmāṇyavāda, opening Vedāntin–Naiyāyika dispute-triad
key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
prāmāṇyaṃ svata iti vedāntinaḥ /
parata iti naiyāyikāḥ //
vedāḥ apauruṣeyā nityāśceti vedāntinaḥ /
īśvaroccaritā anityā iti naiyāyikāḥ //
īśvarasya sārvajñyādikaṃ vedenaiva siddhyatīti vedāntinaḥ /
dharmāgrāhakānumānair iti naiyāyikāḥ //

karaka_structure.note: elliptically supplies the Naiyāyika side of the same predication

key_passages.english_close: Vedāntins say: validity is intrinsic; Naiyāyikas: it is extrinsic. Vedāntins say: the Vedas are authorless and eternal; Naiyāyikas: they are uttered by God and non-eternal. Vedāntins say: the Lord's omniscience and the rest are established by the Veda alone; Naiyāyikas: by inferences that apprehend dharma.
key_passages.why_this_passage: This opening dispute-triad is the clearest available map of the work's argumentative front: intrinsic validity, Vedic authorlessness, and the Veda-grounded proof of a fully qualified Lord. Because the on-disk source is commentary-inclusive, I use this locus explicitly as the file's own framing of Vyāsatīrtha's first pariccheda rather than pretending it is an isolated aphorism torn from context.
key_passages.source_edition: GRETIL plaintext (`sa_vyAsatIrtha-tarkatANDava-comm.txt`), converted 2020-07-31 from the Sansknet Project source; includes Rāghavendra Tīrtha's Nyāyadīpa commentary.

key_passages.locus_long: Tarka-Tāṇḍava, pariccheda 1, prāmāṇyavāda, lakṣaṇa of svatastva in origination

pada_analysis.gloss: origination dependent solely on the producer of the cognition

samasa_vigrahas.resolution: jñānajanakasya mātrasya adhīnaṃ janma yasya tat

key_passages.english_close: Svatastva is origination dependent solely on the producer of the cognition.

preserved_terms.first_use_gloss: intrinsicness, non-derivation from a further external factor

key_passages.why_this_passage: This compressed lakṣaṇa is worth selecting because it states Vyāsatīrtha's positive thesis without dialectical scaffolding: for him, intrinsic validity at the level of origination means that no extra validity-producing factor beyond the cognition's own producer is required. It is not a famous ornamental line but a load-bearing definition inside the prāmāṇyavāda.
key_passages.source_edition: GRETIL plaintext (`sa_vyAsatIrtha-tarkatANDava-comm.txt`), converted 2020-07-31 from the Sansknet Project source; includes Rāghavendra Tīrtha's Nyāyadīpa commentary.

key_passages.locus_long: Tarka-Tāṇḍava, pariccheda 1, prāmāṇyavāda, inference for the intrinsic graspability of cognitive validity
key_passages.sanskrit_iast:
jñānaprāmāṇyaṃ svato grāhyaṃ,
parato 'gṛhyamāṇatve sati gṛhyamāṇatvāt /
yadyad anyenāgṛhyamāṇatve sati grāhyaṃ
tat tena grāhyaṃ,
yathā cakṣur anyenāgṛhyamāṇaṃ rūpaṃ
cakṣurgrāhyam //

pada_analysis.gloss: in the state of not being grasped
in the state of not being grasped

karaka_structure.note: predicate subject of the inferential statement
instrument in the universal concomitance clause
object of the illustrative eye-grasp case
understood instrument in the example; compacted in cakṣurgrāhyam

key_passages.english_close: The validity of a cognition is intrinsically graspable. The reason: while not being grasped by anything else, it is nevertheless grasped. [General rule]: whatever, while not being grasped by another. Is [nevertheless] grasped. That is grasped by that very thing itself. Just as a visible form, not grasped by another instrument. Is grasped by the eye.
key_passages.why_this_passage: This is the work's most explicit compact inferential defense of svato-grāhyatva, and so it shows Vyāsatīrtha doing what makes him distinctive: casting a Dvaita epistemic thesis in sharp Navya-style inferential form. It fairly represents the text because the surrounding section is devoted precisely to defending intrinsic validity against Naiyāyika externalism.
key_passages.source_edition: GRETIL plaintext (`sa_vyAsatIrtha-tarkatANDava-comm.txt`), converted 2020-07-31 from the Sansknet Project source; includes Rāghavendra Tīrtha's Nyāyadīpa commentary.
